sections.paragraph:
f. (from the root שָׁמַם)-

(1) wasting, desolation, Isa 5:9 Jer 2:15 Psa 73:19.

(2) astonishment, Jer 8:21 meton. of its object, Deu 28:37 Jer 19:8 25:9 , 18 Jer 25:18 51:37 .

(3) [Shammah], pr.n. m.-(a) a son of Raguel, Gen 36:13, 17 Gen 36:17.-(b) a son of Jesse, and brother of David, 1Sa 16:9 17:13 called elsewhere שִׁמְעָה 2Sa 13:3, 32 2Sa 13:32and שִׁמְעָא 1Ch 2:13.-(c) 2Sa 23:11.-(d) 2Sa 23:33.-(e) 2Sa 23:25, for which there is שַׁמֹּות [Shammoth], 1Ch 11:27 שַׁמְהוּת [Shamhuth], 1Ch 27:8.
